Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. On September 17, 2014, general elections were held for the 50 members of Parliament in Fiji, with the FijiFirst party led by Prime Minister Frank Bainimarama winning 32 of the 50 seats. The Social Democratic Liberal Party and the National Federation Party were the only two parties to cross the 5% electoral threshold and win seats. The elections were originally planned for March 2009, but were postponed due to politicians not accepting the People's Charter for Change, Peace and Progress. Multiple public announcements and requests were made, and on March 23, 2014, the interim government announced
B. General elections were held in Fiji on 17 September 2014 to select the 50 members of Parliament. The FijiFirst party led by Prime Minister Frank Bainimarama won a landslide victory, winning 32 of the 50 seats. The Social Democratic Liberal Party and the National Federation Party were the only other two parties to cross the 5% electoral threshold and win seats.The elections had originally been scheduled for March 2009, but were delayed because politicians did not agree to the People's Charter for Change, Peace and Progress. Between 2009 and 2014 several public announcements and requests were made for elections, and on 23 March 2014 the interim government announced the elections would be held on 17 September 2014. The elections were to be held under the new constitution which
C. On September 17, 2014, Fiji conducted general elections to elect the 50 members of its Parliament. The FijiFirst party, under the leadership of Prime Minister Frank Bainimarama, secured a decisive triumph, obtaining 32 out of the 50 available seats. Apart from FijiFirst, only two other political entities, the Social Democratic Liberal Party and the National Federation Party, managed to exceed the 5% electoral threshold and thus secure representation. Initially, these elections were slated for March 2009; however, they experienced a postponement due to a lack of consensus among politicians regarding the People's Charter for Change, Peace and Progress. Over the period spanning 2009 to 2014, numerous public declarations and appeals for elections were issued. Ultimately, on March 23, 2014, the interim government formally declared that the elections would proceed on September 17, 2014. These electoral proceedings were mandated to take place in accordance with the newly established constitution, which

options.C: On September 17, 2014, Fiji conducted general elections to elect the 50 members of its Parliament. The FijiFirst party, under the leadership of Prime Minister Frank Bainimarama, secured a decisive triumph, obtaining 32 out of the 50 available seats. Apart from FijiFirst, only two other political entities, the Social Democratic Liberal Party and the National Federation Party, managed to exceed the 5% electoral threshold and thus secure representation.
